features.name: Global VPC Topologies
features.description: Constructs virtual network topologies globally across GCP, organizing regional subnets in a private boundary.

features.name: High-Speed Interconnect
features.description: Connects on-premises datacenters directly to GCP networks via Dedicated or Partner fiber lines (up to 100 Gbps).

features.name: Dynamic BGP Routing
features.description: Exchanges route tables dynamically between Cloud Routers and on-premises networks using dynamic routing.

iamRoles.description: Full access to create, modify, and delete networking resources, VPCs, subnets, and firewalls.
Read-only access to VPC topologies, routing configurations, and IP allocations.

cliExamples.description: Create a custom Virtual Private Cloud (VPC) network
Create a regional subnet inside a VPC network
cliExamples.command:
gcloud compute networks subnets create vpc-flow-logs-subnet \
  --network=my-custom-vpc \
  --region=us-central1 \
  --range=10.10.1.0/24 \
  --enable-private-ip-google-access

design: VPC networks are global resources; subnets are regional resources hosting virtual machines and network interfaces.
Shared VPC allows a host project to share subnets with service projects, centralizing network control.
VPC Network Peering connects two VPCs securely with low latency; routing is non-transitive.
Cloud Router uses Border Gateway Protocol (BGP) to exchange routes dynamically between VPCs and external systems.
HA VPN deploys two active tunnels with dynamic routing to guarantee a 99.99% connection uptime SLA.
Cloud Interconnect provides physical fiber connections; Dedicated connects directly, Partner connects via providers.
VPC network peering requires that IP CIDR blocks do not overlap between the peered networks.
Configure Private Google Access on subnets to allow VMs with only internal IPs to access Google APIs.
security: Firewall rules are stateful and applied to VM instances using network tags, service accounts, or IP ranges.
Hierarchical firewall policies apply network rules at the Organization or Folder levels, enforcing corporate standards.
Cloud NAT allows private VMs to access the internet for updates while blocking incoming connections.
Audit network connections, blocked ports, and packets using VPC Flow Logs.
Enable Firewall Rules Logging to audit which rules are actively blocking or allowing traffic.
Secure hybrid connections by encrypting Cloud Interconnect traffic using MACsec or IPsec VPN tunnels.
Use Private Service Connect to expose internal services to other VPCs privately without IP peering.
Restrict administrative network changes using the networkAdmin IAM role.
reliability: HA VPN requires deploying two tunnels from different gateway interfaces to distinct public peer IPs.
Cloud Router automatically handles path failover by dynamically updating routing paths via BGP.
Dedicated Interconnect offers 99.99% availability when deployed with dual circuits across two edge locations.
Replicate subnets across multiple zones in a region to ensure high availability for compute nodes.
Configure health checks on load balancers to route traffic away from unhealthy network zones.
VPC networks support automatic subnet routing, which propagates routes globally.
Monitor network latency and packet drops using Network Intelligence Center.
Dynamic routing mode must be set to 'Global' to allow routers to propagate routes across all regions.
cost: VPC networks do not charge for network creation; billing is based on network data transfer (egress).
Data transfer within the same zone is free, but cross-zone transfer within the region incurs network fees.
Cross-region data egress is billed based on source and destination region rates; design zones carefully.
Cloud NAT charges per gateway hour and for data processed; do not deploy NAT gateways in idle subnets.
Dedicated Interconnect reduces overall cross-region egress billing compared to standard internet egress.
Prune unused static external IP addresses to avoid hourly idle reservation fees.
VPC Flow Logs generate large volumes of metadata; configure sampling rates to control logging costs.
Use Shared VPC to consolidate network infrastructure, reducing the number of VPN gateways and NATs needed.
